description: Add a unit test that specifically validates 500 errors in the usage refresh path keep eligible connections clean (same as the request path), to prevent regression.
steps: Create a new test file tests/unit/usage-refresh-5xx-transient.test.ts that tests the usage refresh fallback handling for 5xx errors.
The test should mock the same modules as tests/unit/status-handling-transient-auth.test.ts (use it as a pattern reference) but focus on the connectionUsageRefresh error handling path.

Test cases to include:
  1. Status 500 from usage API keeps eligible connection clean (routingStatus stays 'eligible', no healthStatus:'degraded', no reasonCode, no nextRetryAt)
  2. Status 503 from usage API keeps eligible connection clean
  3. Status 429 from usage API keeps eligible connection clean (no nextRetryAt)
  4. Status 500 from usage API on a non-eligible (e.g. already blocked) connection writes degraded with transient_upstream_error
  5. Non-5xx non-auth error (e.g. status 400 that is not auth-related) correctly blocks the connection
  6. Verify isConfirmedAuthBlockedError is NOT called in the fallback path (dead code removed)
Run the new test to verify it passes: bun vitest run --reporter=verbose --root tests tests/unit/usage-refresh-5xx-transient.test.ts
acceptance_criteria: New test file tests/unit/usage-refresh-5xx-transient.test.ts exists and passes
Tests cover all 5xx codes (500, 503) keeping eligible connections clean
Tests cover 429 keeping eligible connections clean without nextRetryAt
Tests cover non-eligible connections getting degraded status for 5xx
Tests cover genuinely unknown errors (non-5xx, non-auth) producing blocked
findings: All 5 tests pass. The key insight is that connections need authType:'oauth' to bypass the early skip in loadUsageConnection. The test exercises the full refreshConnectionUsage flow by mocking getUsageForProvider to throw errors with specific .status codes, verifying the patch passed to syncUsageStatus matches expectations.
